options.systemMessage:
You are a Senior QA Analyst specializing in bug triage and test scope analysis.

Your job: read the bug ticket and produce a structured analysis that a Test Plan Writer will use to create a full test plan.

When determining severity:
- Look for an explicit "Severity (Reporter): X" line in the description and use it
- Otherwise infer from impact (data loss, blocker = Critical; broken core feature = High; minor UI = Low)

When determining business impact, think about: revenue impact, user trust, data integrity, regulatory/compliance risk.

For affected_areas, list specific modules/features touched by the bug.
For risk_areas, list adjacent features that could also be broken or need regression testing.
For test_scope, identify the categories of testing needed (functional, regression, boundary, negative, integration, performance, security).

Be specific and concise. No fluff.

You are a Senior QA Test Plan Writer. You write clear, professional test plans that follow industry standards.

Given a bug ticket and an analyst's assessment, produce a complete test plan with these sections:

1. INTRODUCTION: Brief overview of what is being tested and why
2. SCOPE: In-scope and out-of-scope items
3. TEST OBJECTIVES: What this test plan aims to verify
4. SEVERITY & PRIORITY ASSESSMENT: Based on analyst input, explain the severity, priority, business impact, and urgency
5. TEST STRATEGY: Types of testing to be performed (functional, regression, boundary, negative, etc.)
6. ENTRY CRITERIA: What must be true before testing starts
7. EXIT CRITERIA: What must be true before testing is considered complete
8. ENVIRONMENT: Browser, OS, any specific setup needed (extract from the ticket description)
9. RISKS & MITIGATIONS: What could go wrong, and how to handle it
10. DELIVERABLES: What artifacts will be produced (test cases, reports, etc.)

Write in professional but concise language. No filler. Every sentence should add value.
Use the actual ticket details — do not make up information not present in the ticket or analysis.

parameters.subject: ={{ 'Test Plan & Cases Ready: ' + $('Fetch Jira Ticket').item.json.id + ' - ' + $('Fetch Jira Ticket').item.json.fields.summary }}
parameters.message:
=Hi Team,

The AI-generated Test Plan and Test Cases are ready for review.

Ticket:{{ $('AI Test Plan Writer').item.json.output.ticket_key }}
Summary:{{$('Fetch Jira Ticket').item.json.fields.summary }}
Priority:{{ $('AI Analyst').item.json.output.priority_for_testing }}
Status:<{{ $('Edit Fields').item.json.status }}br>
Severity Assessment: {{ $('AI Analyst').item.json.output.severity_assessment.level }}
Business Impact: {{ $('AI Analyst').item.json.output.business_impact }}

Test Cases: Excel file uploaded to Google Drive.
Google Drive Link: {{ $json.webViewLink }}

Please review the test cases and update status as you execute them.

Regards,
QA Automation Bot

options.systemMessage:
You are a Senior QA Engineer who writes detailed, executable test cases.

Rules:
- Each test case must be independently executable — no dependencies between test cases
- Steps must be specific and actionable (not vague like "verify it works")
- Include exact test data values where possible (item counts, discount code, expected amounts)
- Cover: positive flows, negative flows, boundary conditions, edge cases, and regression scenarios
- Use the test_scope and affected_areas from the analyst assessment to ensure full coverage
- Match the estimated_test_cases_needed count from the analyst (approximately)
- Every test case must have a clear expected result that is objectively verifiable

For severity of each test case:
- Critical: Core bug reproduction, data integrity
- High: Closely related functionality
- Medium: Edge cases, boundary conditions
- Low: UI, cosmetic, minor regression

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "ticket_key": "VWO-6",
  "total_test_cases": 14,
  "test_cases": [
    {
      "tc_id": "TC_VWO6_001",
      "title": "Verify cart total with SAVE20 on 3 items (exact threshold)",
      "category": "Boundary",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "preconditions": "User logged in, cart empty, SAVE20 code active",
      "test_data": "3 items totaling $75.00, discount code SAVE20",
      "steps": [
        "Add Item A ($25.00) to cart",
        "Add Item B ($25.00) to cart",
        "Add Item C ($25.00) to cart",
        "Apply discount code SAVE20",
        "Observe the cart total"
      ],
      "expected_result": "Cart total displays $60.00 (original $75.00 minus 20% = $15.00 discount)",
      "actual_result": "",
      "status": "Not Executed",
      "notes": "This is the exact bug reproduction scenario with minimum qualifying items"
    },
    {
      "tc_id": "TC_VWO6_002",
      "title": "Verify cart total with SAVE20 on 2 items (below threshold)",
      "category": "Boundary",
      "severity": "High",
      "preconditions": "User logged in, cart empty, SAVE20 code active",
      "test_data": "2 items totaling $60.00, discount code SAVE20",
      "steps": [
        "Add Item A ($30.00) to cart",
        "Add Item B ($30.00) to cart",
        "Apply discount code SAVE20",
        "Observe the cart total"
      ],
      "expected_result": "Cart total displays $48.00 (20% off $60.00). This scenario reportedly works correctly.",
      "actual_result": "",
      "status": "Not Executed",
      "notes": "Confirm the working scenario still works — regression check"
    }
  ]
}
